title: Tutte's Flow Conjectures (Conjecture 1.2)
statement_text:
Let $G$ be an oriented 2-edge-connected graph.
1. $G$ has a nowhere-zero 5-flow.
2. If $G$ does not have a Petersen graph minor, it has a nowhere-zero 4-flow.
3. If $G$ is 4-edge-connected, it has a nowhere-zero 3-flow.
context_text: Tutte initiated the study of nowhere-zero flows and observed their duality to colorings in planar graphs. He made three conjectures that have directed the field; despite a wealth of research, all three remain open.
paper_contribution: Cited as open background motivation. The paper does not directly advance these conjectures but instead proves exponentially many nowhere-zero flows exist under related edge-connectivity hypotheses, extending results of Seymour and Jaeger.
notes: Three sub-conjectures (5-flow, 4-flow, 3-flow) are grouped under a single labeled environment Conjecture 1.2 in the paper. References given as [13, 15, 3]; year not recoverable from the extracted text.
paper_title: Many flows in the group connectivity setting

title: Conjecture 1.10
statement_text: There exists a fixed constant $c > 1$ so that the following holds. For every 3-edge-connected oriented graph $G = (V, E)$ of order $n$, every abelian group $\Gamma$ with $|\Gamma| \geq 6$, and every $f : E \to \Gamma$, there exist at least $c^n$ flows $\phi : E \to \Gamma$ with $\phi(e) \neq f(e)$ for every $e \in E$.
context_text: Theorem 1.9 establishes an explicit exponential lower bound on the number of $\Gamma$-connected flows for groups of size $k \geq 8$, but yields only the existence of a single flow for $k = 6, 7$. The authors attribute this gap to a shortcoming of their techniques and conjecture the exponential bound holds for all abelian groups of size at least 6. Theorem 1.9 confirms the conjecture for all groups except $\mathbb{Z}_6$ and $\mathbb{Z}_7$; Theorem 1.11 gives a superpolynomial (but not exponential) lower bound for $\mathbb{Z}_6$.
paper_title: Many flows in the group connectivity setting
